name: On the shores of the Tranco de Beas reservoir from Hornos - circular through the Cazorla, Segura and Las Villas Sierras National Park
name_original: A orillas del embalse de Tranco de Beas desde Hornos - circular por el P. N. de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas
region_intro: Mountain biking around Hornos offers a diverse landscape within the Cazorla, Segura and Las Villas Sierras National Park. The region is characterized by mountainous terrain, dense forests, and the presence of the Tranco de Beas reservoir. Riders can expect varied elevations and natural trails suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Hornos The most popular mountain bike trail is On the shores of the Tranco de Beas reservoir from Hornos - circular through the Cazorla, Segura and Las Villas Sierras National Park, a difficult 45.2 miles (72.7 km) trail that takes 5 hours 40 minutes to complete. This route offers extensive views of the reservoir and surrounding parkland. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Risca del Guijarrón desde el charco del Aceite - circular por el P. N. de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas, a difficult 19.4 miles (31.3 km) path. This circular route explores the natural park's rugged terrain and offers challenging climbs. Local mountain bikers also love the C.F. El Campillo Shelter – Río Madera Inn and Lodging loop from Camping Garrote Gordo, a 32.9 miles (53.0 km) trail leading through forested areas and river valleys, often completed in about 4 hours 37 minutes. Mountain biking around Hornos is defined by mountainous landscapes, dense pine forests, and reservoir-side paths. The network offers options for easy mountain bike trails Hornos as well as advanced mountain bike routes Hornos. The routes in Hornos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Hornos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Hornos?
A: There are over 40 mountain bike trails documented on komoot around Hornos, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. The region is known for its mountainous terrain and dense forests within the Cazorla, Segura and Las Villas Sierras National Park.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails in Hornos suitable for beginners?
A: While the majority of trails around Hornos are rated as moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy route available. For beginners, it's advisable to check the route details carefully on komoot to ensure it matches your skill level and fitness.

Q: What are the typical lengths of mountain bike trails in this region?
A: Trail lengths vary significantly. For example, the difficult route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993712893/risca-del-guijarron-desde-el-charco-del-aceite-circular-por-el-p-n-de-las-sierras-de-cazorla-segura-y-las-villas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Risca del Guijarrón desde el charco del Aceite&lt;/a&gt; is about 31 km, while the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993709611/on-the-shores-of-the-tranco-de-beas-reservoir-from-hornos-circular-through-the-cazorla-segura-and-las-villas-sierras-national-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;On the shores of the Tranco de Beas reservoir from Hornos&lt;/a&gt; extends to over 72 km. You'll find options ranging from shorter loops to longer, more demanding expeditions.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Hornos?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Hornos are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33712981" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;C.F. El Campillo Shelter – Río Madera Inn and Lodging loop from Camping Garrote Gordo&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a scenic circular journey through forested areas and river valleys.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on the mountain bike trails in Hornos?
A: The trails offer diverse and spectacular views, including extensive vistas of the Tranco de Beas reservoir, rugged mountainous landscapes, and dense pine forests. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38215006" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Yelmo Summit (1,808 m) – El Robledo Viewpoint loop&lt;/a&gt; provide panoramic views from higher elevations.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or natural attractions to see while mountain biking in Hornos?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and points of interest. You can encounter landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5298253" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Yelmo Summit (1,808 m)&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5446556" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Risca del Guijarrón&lt;/a&gt; viewpoint, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2064086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Segura River&lt;/a&gt;. The trails often pass through or near these natural highlights.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Hornos?
A: The mountain bike trails around Hornos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, the stunning natural beauty of the Cazorla, Segura and Las Villas Sierras National Park, and the challenging yet rewarding climbs and descents.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that pass by historical sites or villages?
A: Yes, some routes offer glimpses of local culture and history. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40335465" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Segura de la Sierra – Segura de la Sierra loop&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the historic Castle of Segura de la Sierra, offering a blend of natural and cultural exploration.

Q: What is the best season for mountain biking in Hornos?
A: The Cazorla, Segura and Las Villas Sierras National Park generally offers good conditions for mountain biking during spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, especially at lower elevations, while winters may bring colder temperatures and occasional snow at higher altitudes. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: What should I bring for a mountain biking trip in Hornos?
A: Given the mountainous terrain and varied trail lengths, it's essential to be well-prepared. Always carry sufficient water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike, a first-aid kit, and appropriate clothing layers. A helmet is mandatory, and navigation tools like komoot are highly recommended, as some areas can be remote.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that are particularly challenging for advanced riders?
A: Hornos is well-suited for advanced riders, with 28 out of 40 trails rated as difficult. These routes often feature significant elevation gains, technical descents, and long distances. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993709611/on-the-shores-of-the-tranco-de-beas-reservoir-from-hornos-circular-through-the-cazorla-segura-and-las-villas-sierras-national-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;On the shores of the Tranco de Beas reservoir from Hornos&lt;/a&gt; route, at over 72 km with substantial climbing, is a prime example of a demanding trail.
tips: It is a track in good condition that runs along the edge of the Tranco reservoir. Ideal for a stop at the river beaches and take a dip.
The Mirabueno viewpoint does not have this name by chance. You are correct because the views over the Guadalquivir river are excellent.

The Cabeza de la Viña Viewpoint is located in the Tranco reservoir, on the peak of the Cabeza de la Viña Island, center of the Bujaraiza Reserve Area. It can only be accessed on foot when the waters of the reservoir are low, since the Island is isolated by the waters most of the year, times when it can be reached by boat without a motor. Once on the Island you have to climb to its summit; there is a forest track that surrounds the island and ascends to a maximum point of 832 m, in the Collado between the Island and Mount Cabeza de la Viña, from where it is accessed by a path.

Hornos is a town and municipality in the province of Jaén, in the autonomous community of Andalusia, belonging to the Comarca de Segura. It has a surface area of 118.4 km². According to INE sources, in 2016 it had a population of 649 inhabitants. 

Its municipal area belongs entirely to the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura and Las Villas natural park, including the enclave of Cabeza de la Viña nestled within the Santiago-Pontones area. 

The Hornos de Segura Castle, dating from the 12th to the 14th centuries, dominates the town from its rocky watchtower, and is the main reference point of the historic-artistic complex along with the Church of Our Lady of the Assumption (16th century), the Gate of the Town and the Fort (of Roman origin).
